purpose: Public SAMPLE asset for the binding v0.9.0 naive-agent README runnability gate. A clean-room agent clones the public tag, follows the README only, downloads this asset, runs ONE forecast hour through the JAX GPU port, and dimension-compares the generated wrfout against the packaged CPU-WRF reference.

branch_base.note: The gpuwrf CLI is owned by the worker/opus/readme-runnability lane and is NOT on trunk-0.9.0 yet; it must be merged to the tagged commit at release time. The dry-check below ran the readme-runnability cli.py against the trunk-0.9.0 gpuwrf package.

case.description: Canary Islands d02 (3 km nest) CPU-WRF / Gen2 operational backfill case. Complete 72 h hourly run; sample packages the first 3 hourly d02 frames (init + 2 h) so a 1 h replay forecast can run and dimension-compare.

asset.commit_note: Asset is 66.5 MiB; staged under release-assets/ but NOT committed to git (too large; .gitignored). At tag time the manager uploads it as a GitHub release asset and pins the URL + this sha256 in the README.

contents.name: namelist.input
contents.role: GPU-port namelist (validated fail-closed by the CLI; the file the CLI consumes as <input-dir>/namelist.input)

contents.name: namelist.input.cpu-wrf-original
contents.role: Verbatim CPU-WRF namelist that produced the reference wrfout (provenance only)

contents.name: wrfout_d02_2026-04-29_19:00:00
contents.role: CPU-WRF d02 history t=1h (replay boundary + the 1h dimension-compare reference)

contents.name: wrfout_d02_2026-04-29_20:00:00
contents.role: CPU-WRF d02 history t=2h (replay boundary margin)

expected_output.stdout: one JSON pipeline payload; dimension_compare_status=PASS; process exit code 0
expected_output.pass_criterion: every NetCDF dimension of the generated d02 wrfout matches the packaged CPU-WRF reference of the same basename exactly (dims listed in case.dims_d02)

deviation_from_cpu_wrf_namelist.why: The raw CPU-WRF namelist sets diff_opt=1/km_opt=4, which the GPU port's fail-closed registry rejects (diff_opt in {0,2}, km_opt in {0,1}). The GPU operational pipeline (daily_pipeline._build_real_case) does NOT read these options from the namelist: it runs OperationalNamelist with diff_opt=0/km_opt=0 and applies horizontal filtering via diff_6th_opt=2/diff_6th_factor=0.12 (kept identical). Setting them to 0 in the sample therefore reflects exactly what the GPU port runs and is the honest, faithful value. This does not affect the dimension compare (dims are fixed by e_we/e_sn/e_vert, unchanged). The original is preserved as namelist.input.cpu-wrf-original.
deviation_from_cpu_wrf_namelist.honesty_note: The CPU-WRF reference wrfout WAS produced with diff_opt=1/km_opt=4. The dimension gate is unaffected; a future VALUE/RMSE comparison must account for this scheme difference (the GPU port does not implement km_opt=4 3D-TKE diffusion).

tag_time_manager_steps: Merge worker/opus/readme-runnability (gpuwrf CLI + README) into the tagged commit so `gpuwrf run` exists at the tag.
Upload release-assets/gpuwrf-canary-d02-sample.tgz as a GitHub release asset on the v0.9.0 tag at github.com/wrf-gpu/wrf_gpu.
Pin the resolved download URL AND sha256 1260f182e47d84788f67a21aa1eb5426aacfe0e91d8ee684120b8e8052aad17a in the README (replace asset.download_url TODO placeholder).

Run the ACTUAL gate on a GPU: extract the asset, `pip install -e .`, run the gate_command.exact above, and confirm runs/canary_d02_sample/proofs/dimension_compare.json status=PASS and CLI exit code 0. THIS GPU run is the binding gate; this lane only did CPU/IO selection + packaging + a no-GPU structural dry-check.
(Optional, post-0.9.0) Extend the gate from dimension-compare to a value/RMSE comparison, accounting for the diff_opt/km_opt scheme difference.
